purpose: Research-backed implementation buildbook for turning Kolm's product-surface map into a finished backend system: build strategy, capture, privacy, distillation, quantization, compilation, runtime, cloud compute, evals, registry, agents, SDKs, and proof.
source_policy: Prefer primary papers, official runtime/provider documentation, and official security/compliance specifications.
Treat external package releases, public benchmarks, external runtime adoption, and live certifications as honest gates until real external evidence exists.
Every invention must map to product journeys, customization dimensions, readiness requirements, metrics, implementation files, acceptance tests, and a local smoke simulation.

research_sources.lesson: Learned rotations can reduce outliers before low-bit weight, activation, and KV-cache quantization; Kolm should search rotation choices when a target requires 4-bit serving without sacrificing the held-out K-score.
W4A8KV4 serving only helps when kernels and quantization are co-designed; Kolm should emit runtime-specific quantization plans rather than generic INT4 labels.
TensorRT-LLM exposes quantization, guided decoding, speculative decoding, and production serving paths; Kolm should lower artifact runtime policies to this family when NVIDIA GPU serving is selected.
Speculative decoding has framework-specific constraints and configuration shape; Kolm receipts should record the accepted draft/target policy and rejected speculative paths.
SGLang's EAGLE-based speculative decoding and runtime flags show that serving strategy is a tunable product decision, not a static model property.
RadixAttention-style KV-cache reuse matters for structured generation programs; Kolm should map repeated prompt/tool flows to prefix/KV reuse policies.
IREE treats deployment as a compiler configuration problem; Kolm's compiler target should emit explicit lowering configs and unsupported-feature diagnostics.
A retargetable MLIR compiler/runtime can span datacenter to mobile/edge; Kolm should keep a target matrix instead of hard-coding a single runtime path.
ONNX Runtime execution providers allocate supported subgraphs to hardware-specific backends; Kolm should validate provider support and receipt the execution provider used.
GenAI semantic conventions define shared traces, metrics, logs, model spans, agent spans, and provider attributes; Kolm capture/runtime receipts should emit compatible telemetry.
GenAI span guidance warns against capturing inputs/outputs by default; Kolm should make content capture opt-in and policy-controlled while still emitting safe metadata.
Signing and verification need easy tooling and public-good transparency patterns; Kolm artifact verification should work without the full hosted control plane.
SLSA provenance makes build integrity auditable; Kolm should generate artifact provenance with builder identity, source hashes, dependencies, and reproducibility claims.
R2 presigned URLs provide S3-compatible direct upload/download without exposing credentials; Kolm cloud jobs should use short-lived signed object paths for datasets and artifacts.
RunPod exposes pods, serverless endpoints, autoscaling jobs, and templates; Kolm should model managed GPU compute as a typed job backend with health/fallback semantics.
AWS Batch GPU jobs require explicit GPU families and job definitions; Kolm BYOC execution should emit concrete queue/job definitions instead of vague cloud promises.
Kueue provides Kubernetes-native quota-aware batch scheduling; Kolm enterprise installs should map long compiles/training to queueable jobs with admission state.
Predibase combines LoRA adapters and speculative serving, setting the bar for adapter-first UX; Kolm must beat this by adding signed artifacts, offline portability, and proof.
OpenPipe's expensive-prompt-to-cheap-model wedge validates capture-to-fine-tune demand; Kolm should provide the same loop while adding stronger eval, artifact, and runtime controls.
Braintrust treats evaluations as immutable comparable experiments; Kolm K-score and compile receipts should expose immutable eval snapshots and CI comparison hooks.
LangSmith combines observability, evaluation, prompt engineering, and deployment; Kolm should integrate with that mental model but make promotion gates artifact-native.
Conformal intervals expose judge uncertainty instead of pretending a single judge score is deterministic; Kolm should make K-score intervals, abstention, and coverage explicit.
Calibrated surrogate metrics and doubly robust inference reduce biased off-policy evaluation; Kolm should report calibration uncertainty and overlap diagnostics for replay-based promotion.
Black-box on-policy distillation reduces mismatch by training from student-generated trajectories with adaptive feedback; Kolm should not rely on static teacher dumps when the task is generative.
Generalized knowledge distillation trains on student distributions to reduce exposure bias; Kolm's distill planner should select on-policy loops when replay traces show distribution shift.
Opacus operationalizes DP training with privacy accounting; Kolm privacy receipts should include clipping, noise, epsilon, delta, and accountant metadata.
Secure aggregation lets participants contribute updates without revealing individual values; Kolm federated compile should receipt participant commitments and aggregation proofs.
MCP creates a standard tool and context interface for agents; Kolm should compile signed, policy-limited MCP tools from proven artifacts.

inventions.name: Adaptive Lattice Quantization Oracle
inventions.thesis: Quantization should be selected by a constrained optimizer over held-out K-score, p95 latency, byte size, memory ceiling, runtime kernel support, and rollback cost; the user should never choose AWQ/GPTQ/SpinQuant/QServe by hand.
build_steps: Load artifact task type, base model family, context length, target hardware, memory ceiling, calibration rows, and K-score floor.
Score candidate methods: no-quant, FP8, AWQ, GPTQ, SmoothQuant, SpinQuant-style rotation, QServe-style W4A8KV4, KV-cache quantization, and GGUF fallback.
Reject candidates whose runtime backend cannot execute the required kernel or whose memory plan exceeds target headroom.
Run a deterministic local calibration simulation when real kernels are unavailable; mark the result synthetic and never claim benchmark evidence.
Emit a quantization plan with selected method, rejected alternatives, expected byte size, expected latency class, calibration hash, and rollback plan.
Write the quantization decision into the .kolm receipt and product graph so CLI, API, account, and docs expose the same answer.
Fail closed when holdout rows are missing, when the method has no runtime verifier, or when the simulated K-score confidence interval crosses the floor.
failure_modes: False speedup caused by selecting INT4 without a kernel that avoids dequantization overhead.
K-score regression hidden by evaluating on training rows instead of holdout rows.
User confusion from exposing low-level quant names without an objective function.

inventions.name: Compiler Swarm Autotarget
inventions.thesis: A real AI compiler needs an explicit lowering matrix from recipe behavior to runtime backends; Kolm should decide between ONNX Runtime EPs, IREE, TensorRT-LLM, GGUF, WASM, edge, and mobile targets with unsupported-feature diagnostics.
build_steps: Represent every runtime target as a capability record with supported modalities, quantization methods, context policies, streaming support, and proof limits.
Add a lowering planner that maps artifact requirements to the smallest viable target set.
Emit compile diagnostics when no target supports an op, tool-call shape, modality, or quantization method.
Expose the selected target and rejected targets through CLI JSON, account UI APIs, OpenAPI, and artifact receipts.
Keep package-release gates honest for WASM, iOS, Android, and SDK depth until package artifacts are actually published.
Add LTS compatibility metadata so artifacts compiled today can declare which runtime lines will keep running them.
Add a conformance fixture for each target class even when the local machine lacks that compiler toolchain.
failure_modes: Marketing claims browser/mobile support before packages are released.
Silent CPU fallback when an execution provider cannot execute the planned graph.
Artifact compiled for a runtime without declaring version support and rollback limits.

inventions.name: Teacher Distillation Forge
inventions.thesis: Distillation should be a strategy planner that chooses prompt-only, RAG, synthetic labeling, SFT, preference tuning, reverse-KL distillation, on-policy distillation, or no-train based on data sufficiency, task type, privacy, teacher availability, and holdout risk.
build_steps: Classify task type, output structure, data volume, label noise, holdout size, privacy policy, and teacher-provider permissions.
Reject training when rows or holdout are insufficient; suggest capture, labeling, or RAG instead of running a misleading compile.
Select teacher providers from Anthropic, OpenAI-compatible endpoints, local models, hosted open models, or customer BYOC endpoints based on policy.
Choose static supervised distillation only when replay traces match deployment distribution.
Choose on-policy or adversarial distillation when the student distribution diverges from captured traffic.
Emit compile failure diagnostics that explain missing examples, noisy labels, ambiguous tasks, insufficient holdout, policy blocks, and compute blocks.
Write selected teacher/provider/model/eval hashes to receipts without leaking secrets.
failure_modes: Training from too little data just because the UI has a train button.
Using a cloud teacher when privacy policy requires local or BYOC execution.
Declaring a compiled artifact ready when the holdout confidence interval is underpowered.

inventions.name: Build Strategy Operating System
inventions.thesis: The product must guide users through a single decision tree from task to capture, RAG, route, train, distill, quantize, compile, cloud compute, or local runtime; all UI surfaces should call the same backend planner.
build_steps: Normalize every entrypoint into the same inputs: task, row count, holdout, privacy policy, model family, runtime target, GPU availability, budget, and deployment target.
Return one recommended next action and the next three safe alternatives.
Block unsafe paths with human-readable reasons and machine-readable blockers.
Emit exact next commands for CLI/TUI/account flows.
Include cloud compute and BYOC options when local hardware is insufficient.
Guarantee no simulated or real secret values are printed in planner output.
Pin the same planner through API, CLI, docs, OpenAPI, account UX, and smoke probes.
acceptance_tests: node cli/kolm.js build plan --task generation --rows 1500 --holdout-pairs 300 --no-local-gpu --params-b 7 --context-tokens 8192 --simulate runpod-r2 --summary
failure_modes: Separate UI branches drift into conflicting recommendations.
Planner recommends training when RAG or routing is safer and cheaper.
Account UI hides the reason a path is blocked.

inventions.name: ReceiptOS Verifiable Runtime
inventions.thesis: Kolm's highest-value backend surface is proof: every artifact, runtime call, compile, quantization, eval, provider call, and registry movement needs a verifiable receipt that works outside the hosted product.
build_steps: Define a receipt envelope with artifact hash, runtime hash, model hash, tokenizer hash, eval hash, calibration hash, target runtime, and signature.
Add standalone verification that does not require authenticated hosted API access.
Emit OpenTelemetry-compatible safe metadata spans for compile, runtime, model calls, tool calls, and registry operations.
Keep prompt/input/output capture disabled unless an explicit policy enables it.
Attach SLSA-style provenance to package and artifact build flows.
Surface receipt verification in CLI, account, docs, SDKs, and registry listing pages.
Treat live certifications as claims gates, not strings that tests should hide.
failure_modes: Receipts only verify through Kolm-hosted API and therefore fail air-gapped procurement.
Telemetry captures sensitive inputs by default.
Public copy claims SOC2/HIPAA/FedRAMP before live evidence exists.

inventions.name: Privacy Membrane Governor
inventions.thesis: Capture is the data flywheel, but regulated buyers will only trust it if zero-retention, capture filtering, redaction, data lineage, RBAC, and privacy accounting are policy-enforced rather than copy claims.
build_steps: Make capture policy explicit per tenant, endpoint, model, route, content type, and error state.
Support zero-retention proxy mode that emits cost/latency metadata without storing content.
Run hybrid deterministic pattern plus ML/NER redaction before persistence when content capture is enabled.
Write event lake schema and export contracts for SQL, JSONL, Parquet-like bundles, and OTEL spans.
Tie team capture to tenant RBAC and audit exports.
Add differential privacy accounting to training and synthetic data paths when enabled.
Show exactly why a row was captured, redacted, excluded, or eligible for compile.
failure_modes: Capture lake stores regulated content despite a zero-retention policy.
Redaction claims high quality without per-class public benchmark evidence.
Team event lake uses app-layer filters but no tenant-level data isolation checks.

inventions.name: Federated Trust Lab
inventions.thesis: Healthcare, finance, legal, and defense buyers need cross-party learning without centralizing raw data; Kolm should make federated compile a receipt-backed workflow with DP accounting, secure aggregation, robust aggregation, and participant policy controls.
build_steps: Model a federated compile round as participants, commitments, privacy policy, clipping, noise, aggregation, rejection, and promotion.
Add per-round receipts containing participant commitments but not raw data.
Support secure aggregation and robust aggregation strategies with deterministic fixture tests.
Tie privacy budgets to tenant policy and artifact lineage.
Expose consortium export packets for legal/procurement review.
Prevent promotion when the client count, privacy budget, or adversarial rejection rate is outside policy.
Document this as an enterprise/regulated workflow until external certification evidence exists.
failure_modes: Federated workflow records raw participant updates instead of commitments.
Privacy budget is not carried into final artifact lineage.
Robust aggregation rejects clients without preserving auditable reasons.

inventions.name: Cloud Compute Control Plane
inventions.thesis: Users without local GPUs still need training, distillation, quantization, compile, benchmark, and deploy; cloud compute must be a typed backend surface spanning local, SSH, BYOC, RunPod, AWS, Kubernetes, R2/S3, provider APIs, and edge runtimes.
build_steps: Represent each compute target with capabilities, secrets required, storage requirements, GPU family, queue semantics, privacy mode, and failure class.
Use R2/S3-compatible presigned URLs for direct dataset/artifact transfer when configured.
Emit RunPod serverless/pod plans for managed GPU workloads and AWS Batch/Kueue plans for BYOC enterprise workloads.
Attach cost estimates, max budget, quota, timeout, and cancellation semantics.
Route no-local-GPU build plans to cloud compute only when credentials and policy permit it.
Emit webhooks for queued, running, failed, complete, promoted, and rollback states.
Never print provider secrets in CLI, logs, receipts, docs, or generated plans.
failure_modes: Cloud plan suggests training but no upload/download object path exists.
No-local-GPU user gets blocked instead of receiving a safe cloud/BYOC plan.
Provider credentials leak through debug output.

inventions.name: Adaptive Eval Lab
inventions.thesis: K-score must evolve from a scalar to a calibrated promotion system with confidence intervals, abstention, judge calibration, deterministic checks, off-policy replay diagnostics, and benchmark evidence separation.
build_steps: Split deterministic evals, LLM-judge evals, human labels, replay checks, and production shadow metrics into separate evidence channels.
Add confidence intervals and abstention to every K-score promotion decision.
Report calibration set hash, alpha, holdout hash, judge version, scorer version, and uncertainty propagation.
Use replay/off-policy diagnostics to warn when captured traffic does not overlap the promoted artifact's target distribution.
Mark public benchmark evidence as absent until an externally reproducible run exists.
Expose failed rows, uncertain rows, label-noise warnings, and next-label suggestions to CLI/account/TUI.
Prevent a compile promotion when confidence bounds or holdout independence fail.
failure_modes: Single judge score is treated as deterministic truth.
Benchmark warnings are removed without public benchmark data.
Off-policy replay is used despite low overlap with target deployment traffic.

inventions.name: Artifact Market Maker
inventions.thesis: The registry should be Docker Hub plus Model Card plus receipt verifier for .kolm artifacts: searchable, versioned, diffable, deployable, private, license-tagged, dependency-aware, and honest about verification status.
build_steps: Index artifacts by task type, model family, runtime target, compliance tag, license, K-score, byte size, publisher, and verification state.
Add version diff over manifest, recipe, data split hashes, runtime target, quantization method, eval set, and signatures.
Add verified publisher and private registry contracts without claiming third-party adoption before it exists.
Attach dependency graphs showing model, tokenizer, runtime, tool, and dataset lineage.
Add deploy buttons that generate concrete target plans rather than fake one-click success.
Expose public spec and conformance fixtures while keeping compiler policy proprietary.
Wire registry state into receipts and account RBAC.
failure_modes: Registry listings imply verified publishers before verification exists.
Version diff omits data or eval split changes.
Deploy buttons create an account flow but no executable deployment plan.

inventions.name: Agent Tool Compiler
inventions.thesis: Agents need signed, deterministic, policy-limited tools; Kolm should compile proven behavior into MCP-compatible tools with schema tests, token budgets, rate limits, fallback chains, receipts, and trace semantics.
build_steps: Add `kolm compile --as-mcp` as a policy-aware artifact target.
Generate MCP tool schemas from recipe inputs/outputs and validate them against held-out examples.
Attach token budgets, cost budgets, allowed tools, fallback chain, and streaming policy.
Emit OpenTelemetry GenAI and agent spans without capturing sensitive content by default.
Sign the tool bundle and allow offline verification.
Add one-click Claude Desktop, Cursor, Continue, Cline, and local MCP config generation only where package/install gates are real.
Pin the same tool compiler path in CLI, SDK, TUI, docs, and registry listing.
failure_modes: Compiled agent tool can call arbitrary tools outside declared policy.
Tool schema passes examples but fails streaming or fallback behavior.
Docs claim one-click installs before package channels exist.

inventions.name: Device Fit Autopilot
inventions.thesis: Device support is not a checkbox; Kolm should inspect memory, CPU/GPU/NPU availability, runtime packages, context length, privacy mode, and modality before recommending local, browser, mobile, edge, server, or cloud execution.
build_steps: Create a local capability probe for RAM, CPU architecture, GPU/NPU hints, browser APIs, and installed runtimes.
Map artifact needs to local, WASM, edge, mobile, server, and cloud targets.
Emit a decision with exact blockers and install/package gate status.
Prefer local execution when privacy policy requires it and the device can satisfy K-score/latency constraints.
Prefer cloud or BYOC when local hardware cannot fit the artifact or compile path.
Keep package release gates explicit for mobile, WASM, and SDK surfaces.
Write device-fit decisions into account/TUI/CLI guidance and runtime receipts.
failure_modes: Browser/mobile/runtime path appears in UI before package release evidence exists.
Runtime silently falls back to CPU and violates latency promise.
User with no GPU gets no path to compile or train.

inventions.name: Codegraph Product Indexer
inventions.thesis: The codebase has too many routes and surfaces for manual confidence; Kolm needs a product graph that indexes every route, CLI command, TUI view, SDK, readiness gate, claim, package, and smoke probe as an always-failing-on-drift backend contract.
build_steps: Index every route, CLI command, TUI view, SDK package, account tab, smoke probe, readiness item, and public claim.
Assign every indexed node to a product journey and customization dimension.
Fail verification when a route/command exists without a journey owner or smoke probe.
Fail verification when a public claim exceeds the evidence state.
Emit a product graph artifact for frontend/account/TUI use.
Expose focused reports for backend, frontend, docs, SDK, package, and external-evidence lanes.
Use the graph as the primary coordination contract while parallel agents work.
failure_modes: A new route ships with no product owner, docs, or smoke probe.
Frontend copy claims a readiness item the backend marks external.
Parallel agents edit routes and product graph independently.

inventions.name: Serving Runtime Optimizer
inventions.thesis: High-volume inference quality is decided by batching, KV-cache policy, prefix reuse, streaming semantics, semantic cache, fallback chains, and runtime-specific decoding; Kolm should produce a serving plan with measured constraints and safe fallbacks.
build_steps: Plan request path by artifact, runtime target, provider, batching policy, context window, streaming need, and privacy mode.
Add prefix/KV reuse policy when prompt structure is stable and storage policy allows it.
Add semantic cache only when task semantics, privacy mode, and proof requirements permit reuse.
Emit fallback chain with allowed providers/runtimes and quality floor.
Attach rate limits, quotas, cost attribution, and timeout policies per artifact.
Record runtime telemetry opt-in state and safe OTEL spans.
Fail closed when streaming/fallback/cache behavior is not represented in the receipt.
failure_modes: Cache serves content across privacy boundaries.
Fallback provider violates compliance policy.
Streaming output cannot be reconciled with receipt hashes.
